sanskrit: अनन्यचेताः सततं यो मां स्मरति नित्यशः । तस्याहं सुलभः पार्थ नित्ययुक्तस्य योगिनः ॥
hindiTranslation: हे पृथानन्दन  अनन्यचित्तवाला जो मनुष्य मेरा नित्यनिरन्तर स्मरण करता है, उस नित्ययुक्त योगीके लिये मैं सुलभ हूँ अर्थात् उसको सुलभतासे प्राप्त हो जाता हूँ ॥
englishTranslation: Arjuna, for the one whose mind is wholly on me and who remembers me constantly, I am easy to reach.
essence: Constant remembrance makes the divine easy to reach.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: A mind that stays focused on Krishna and remembers him continually finds him easy to reach.
simpleInsight: Devotion does not have to be dramatic. Steady remembering itself creates closeness.
detailedMeaning: On the battlefield of Kurukshetra, Arjuna is being taught how to meet death without fear. After describing disciplined leaving of the body, Krishna adds the heart of devotion: one-pointed remembrance makes him easy to attain.
modernRelevance: You keep checking your phone before a hard conversation, trying to steady yourself. The mind that keeps returning to what matters becomes less scattered and more able to meet the moment.
translationLiteral: अनन्यचेताः (one-pointed in mind) / सततम् (constantly) / यः (who) / माम् (me) / स्मरति (remembers) / नित्यशः (always) / तस्य (for that one) / अहम् (I) / सुलभः (easy to reach) / पार्थ (Arjuna) / नित्ययुक्तस्य (of the ever-joined) / योगिनः (of the yogi)
insight: It shifts devotion from occasional feeling to continuous remembrance.

quoteVariants.text: Keep your mind on me. I become easy to reach.
I am closest to the mind that returns to me.
Remember me. I am easy to reach.
